source_rationale: PRM1, PRM2, and TNP1 are protamines and transition proteins that replace histones during spermatid chromatin condensation. TSSK6 and SPATA3 are also spermiogenesis markers, indicating late-stage spermatids.

VWF, CD34, TIE1, AQP1, and NOSTRIN are canonical endothelial cell markers. VWF and TIE1 are highly specific to vascular endothelium.

PDGFRB, RGS5, NOTCH3, NG2 (CSPG4), MCAM, and ABCC9 are canonical pericyte markers. The presence of GUCY1A1/A2 and IGFBP7 further supports a perivascular mural cell identity.

GFRA1, ZBTB16 (PLZF), UTF1, FGFR3, L1TD1, UCHL1, FMR1, and MORC1 are the most canonical SSC markers. GFRA1 and ZBTB16 are definitive markers of the self-renewing spermatogonial stem cell pool.

SYCP2, SYCP3, SYCE1, SYCE2 are synaptonemal complex components. PRDM9, MEIOB, ZCWPW1, SPATA22, and HLTF are meiotic recombination factors. TEX101 and LY6K mark pachytene-stage spermatocytes undergoing crossover resolution.
